name: Sherlock Holmes
description: Arthur Conan Doyle's consulting detective — his novels, stories, and modern adaptations

questions.q: Who is the author of the Sherlock Holmes stories?

source.notes: Doyle wrote 4 novels and 56 short stories featuring Holmes.

questions.q: What is Sherlock Holmes's London address?

source.notes: A museum at that address in London today commemorates the character.

questions.q: Who is Sherlock Holmes's loyal companion and the narrator of most of the stories?

source.notes: An army doctor wounded in Afghanistan before meeting Holmes.

questions.q: Which musical instrument does Holmes famously play?

source.notes: Stradivarius, according to 'The Adventure of the Cardboard Box'.

questions.q: Which style of hat is most iconically associated with Sherlock Holmes?

source.notes: Popularized by illustrator Sidney Paget; never explicitly named in the text.

questions.q: Who is Holmes's brilliant and elusive older brother?

source.notes: Works in obscure government roles at the Diogenes Club.

questions.q: Who serves as Holmes's arch-nemesis and 'Napoleon of Crime'?

source.notes: Mathematics professor turned criminal mastermind.

questions.q: In what year was the first Sherlock Holmes story published?

source.notes: Beeton's Christmas Annual of 1887.

questions.q: What is the title of the first Sherlock Holmes novel?

source.notes: Conan Doyle was paid £25 for the rights.

questions.q: Which actor plays Sherlock Holmes in the BBC's 2010 modern-day series Sherlock?

source.notes: Series co-created by Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss.

questions.q: Who plays Dr. Watson to Benedict Cumberbatch's Sherlock?

source.notes: Freeman also played Bilbo Baggins in The Hobbit trilogy.

questions.q: In the CBS series Elementary, Dr. Joan Watson is played by which actress?

source.notes: Jonny Lee Miller plays Sherlock in the series.

questions.q: Where is the CBS series Elementary set?

source.notes: Holmes is portrayed as a recovering heroin addict.

questions.q: Which 2009 film starring Robert Downey Jr. reinvented Sherlock Holmes as an action hero?

source.notes: Downey Jr. won a Golden Globe for Best Actor in a Musical/Comedy.

questions.q: Who directed the 2009 Sherlock Holmes and its 2011 sequel, A Game of Shadows?

source.notes: Jude Law played Watson opposite Downey Jr.

questions.q: In which short story did Conan Doyle originally kill off Sherlock Holmes?
choices: The Adventure of the Empty House

source.notes: Published 1893; Holmes returned in 'The Adventure of the Empty House' (1903).

questions.q: Over which dramatic Alpine landmark does Holmes apparently plunge with Moriarty in 'The Final Problem'?

source.notes: Located near Meiringen, Switzerland.

questions.q: Which Edinburgh physician is said to have inspired Conan Doyle's creation of Sherlock Holmes?

source.notes: Bell taught Conan Doyle at Edinburgh's medical school in the 1870s.

questions.q: Who is the only woman Holmes is said to have genuinely admired?

source.notes: Appears in 'A Scandal in Bohemia'; Holmes calls her simply 'the woman'.

questions.q: How many of Conan Doyle's four Holmes novels contain a long flashback set in North America?

source.notes: A Study in Scarlet (Utah Mormons) and The Valley of Fear (Pennsylvania).

questions.q: In which English county is 'The Hound of the Baskervilles' primarily set?

source.notes: Set on Dartmoor in Devon.

questions.q: In how many of Conan Doyle's original Holmes stories does the phrase 'Elementary, my dear Watson' appear verbatim?

source.notes: The phrase was popularized by P.G. Wodehouse and the 1929 Basil Rathbone films.
